meta.description: Universal Generation Control Surface — maps behavioral dimensions across task phases and output file types.
meta.changelog: v3.0: Extended for mistral-vibe workbench with AGENT_TOML, SKILL_MD, and SYSTEM_PROMPT_MD profiles.

phase_intent.context_gathering: Ground in source material, constraints, and scope. Minimize speculation. Maximize fidelity to inputs.
phase_intent.drafting: Expression of synthesized content. Maximize thematic coherence and expressive commitment. Lock to established frame.
phase_intent.review: Evaluate against intent and constraints. Break loops, introduce adversarial perspective, consolidate or flag divergence.

AGENT_TOML._purpose: Mistral-vibe agent configuration file. Must be valid TOML with concrete configuration values.
AGENT_TOML._sre_risk: Hard Boundary Enforcement without Gradation — invalid TOML syntax or non-existent tool names break the agent entirely.

Abstraction Level.directive: Generate literal TOML configuration values. No abstractions. active_model must be a specific model string. Tool names must match built-in tools exactly.
Abstraction Level.failure_mode: Abstract configuration values that cannot be parsed or executed.
Abstraction Level.self_check: Is every configuration value concrete and executable?

Imagery Density.directive: Comments factual only. No metaphors. Describe what each configuration section does, not what it represents.
Imagery Density.failure_mode: Comments that decorate rather than inform.
Imagery Density.self_check: Would a DevOps engineer understand each comment without interpretation?

Emotional / Rhetorical Intensity.directive: Flat technical language. Comments describe, never advocate. No emphasis markers.
Emotional / Rhetorical Intensity.failure_mode: Comments that sell the configuration rather than explain it.

Constraint Adherence.directive: TOML syntax must be valid. Tool names must match: bash, read_file, write_file, search_replace, grep, ask_user_question. Permission values must be: always | ask | deny. active_model must reference a configured model.
Constraint Adherence.failure_mode: Invalid TOML or non-existent references that cause runtime failures.
Constraint Adherence.self_check: Does this TOML parse? Do all tool names exist? Are all permission values valid?

Novelty Injection.directive: Follow mistral-vibe TOML structure exactly. Do not invent new configuration sections or keys. MCP server blocks are especially high-risk: only generate [mcp.servers.<name>] blocks for servers the user explicitly confirmed. Do not invent server names, commands, or args. If no MCP servers were confirmed, omit the section entirely. NEGATIVE EXAMPLE — do not generate: [mcp.servers.graphify] command = 'python3' args = ['-m', 'graphify.serve'] unless 'graphify' was explicitly named by the user.
Novelty Injection.failure_mode: Hallucinated MCP server configurations or invented TOML sections that mistral-vibe cannot parse.
Novelty Injection.self_check: Is every [mcp.servers.*] block traceable to a user-confirmed tool name? Is every key in this TOML documented in the agent-template schema?

validation_rules: TOML syntax must be valid (parseable by @iarna/toml)
active_model must be a non-empty string referencing a configured model
All tool names in disabled_tools must match built-in tools: bash, read_file, write_file, search_replace, grep, ask_user_question
All tool permission values must be: always | ask | deny
All timeout values must be positive integers
[agent.capabilities] values must all be boolean
[agent.context].temperature must be a float between 0.0 and 2.0
[agent.context].top_p must be a float between 0.0 and 1.0
[agent.safety].allowed_dirs must be an array of strings (empty array = unrestricted)
[agent.output].format must be one of: markdown | json | plain
[mcp.servers.*].command must be a non-empty string if the section is present
[session].storage_path must be a non-empty string if persist = true
agent.type must be one of: general-purpose | explore | code-reviewer | agentic-software-engineer
recommended_sections: [agent.capabilities] — Tool Access Flags (read, write, bash, web, vision)
[mcp.servers.*] — MCP Tool Integration Blocks (conditional: only if MCP tools answered yes)
[env] — Runtime Environment Variables (LLM_API_KEY, LLM_MODEL, PROJECT_ROOT, PROJECT_TYPE)

SKILL_MD._purpose: Mistral-vibe skill definition with YAML frontmatter and markdown workflow documentation.
SKILL_MD._sre_risk: Competing Interpretations (Split-Brain) — frontmatter metadata conflicts with body content, or workflow has multiple incompatible organizational schemes.

Abstraction Level.directive: Frontmatter metadata must be concrete (skill name, tools, compatibility). Workflow section can use moderate abstraction to organize steps, but each step must be actionable.
Abstraction Level.failure_mode: Philosophical workflow descriptions that don't translate to executable steps.
Abstraction Level.self_check: Could an agent execute each workflow step as written?

Imagery Density.directive: Use concrete examples to illustrate workflow steps. One example per major step maximum. Examples should clarify the step, not decorate it.
Imagery Density.failure_mode: Examples that are metaphorical rather than executable.
Imagery Density.self_check: Can each example be copy-pasted and run?

Narrative / Logical Progression.directive: Workflow must be step-by-step. Number the steps. Make dependencies explicit.
Narrative / Logical Progression.failure_mode: Workflow that can be read in any order, or steps with implicit dependencies.
Narrative / Logical Progression.self_check: Could someone execute this workflow by following the numbers?

Constraint Adherence.directive: YAML frontmatter must match schema: name (kebab-case), description (string), user-invocable (boolean), allowed-tools (array of valid tool names). Workflow must reference only tools listed in allowed-tools.
Constraint Adherence.failure_mode: Frontmatter with missing required fields, or workflow using tools not in allowed-tools.
Constraint Adherence.self_check: Does frontmatter validate? Does workflow use only declared tools?

Novelty Injection.directive: Workflow organization can use one clear structure (sequential, decision-tree, state-machine). Do not mix organizational metaphors.
Novelty Injection.failure_mode: Workflow organized as sequential in one section, decision-tree in another.
Novelty Injection.self_check: Can I describe the workflow structure in one sentence?

SYSTEM_PROMPT_MD._purpose: Custom system prompt for mistral-vibe agent. Defines expertise, communication approach, and behavioral guidelines.
SYSTEM_PROMPT_MD._sre_risk: Manifesto drift (High Exploration + Weak Constraints) — theatrical identity claims instead of professional expertise.

Abstraction Level.directive: TOML metadata blocks ([prompt], [prompt.parameters], [prompt.variables], [validation]) must be concrete and literal — no abstractions, no placeholder strings like '<value>'. Markdown body sections (## System Message, ## Template Body) may use moderate abstraction where the system role is synthesized thematically. The [prompt.variables] block defines typed placeholders; each variable must have a declared type (string | array | boolean | integer). The [[examples]] blocks must contain actual input/output pairs, not descriptions of what examples would look like.
Abstraction Level.failure_mode: TOML blocks with abstract or placeholder values. Variables declared without types. Examples that describe themselves rather than demonstrating the prompt.
Abstraction Level.self_check: Can each TOML value be parsed literally? Does each example pair contain real content that could be used as a few-shot demonstration?

Imagery Density.directive: Domain-appropriate analogies permitted in expertise section. Communication and constraint sections should be plain language.
Imagery Density.failure_mode: Every expertise claim is a metaphor; reader cannot extract concrete knowledge.
Imagery Density.self_check: Can I extract the agent's actual knowledge domains without decoding metaphors?

Emotional / Rhetorical Intensity.directive: The agent should have a distinct, professional voice. Not flat, but the voice should serve the reader, not perform for them. No ALL CAPS.
Emotional / Rhetorical Intensity.failure_mode: Prompt reads as marketing copy or manifesto.
Emotional / Rhetorical Intensity.self_check: Does the tone feel earned by the domain, or borrowed from a dramatic context?

Novelty Injection.directive: Express the agent's expertise distinctly in the ## System Message section. Variable names in [prompt.variables] must follow snake_case and be referenced consistently as {{variable_name}} in the markdown body. Do not invent variable types beyond: string | array | boolean | integer. Category values in [prompt] must be one of: extraction | analysis | generation | classification | transformation | validation.
Novelty Injection.failure_mode: Invented variable types. Category values not in the defined enum. Variable names used in the body that do not appear in [prompt.variables].
Novelty Injection.self_check: Is every {{variable}} in the markdown body declared in [prompt.variables]? Are all category and type values from the allowed lists?

Perspective Stability.directive: Write in first person ('I am a security expert'). Maintain consistent voice across all sections.
Perspective Stability.failure_mode: Voice shifts make the prompt feel fragmented or uncertain.
Perspective Stability.self_check: Is the first-person voice consistent throughout?

validation_rules: TOML blocks must be valid and parseable
[prompt].name must be kebab-case
[prompt].version must be valid semver
[prompt].category must be one of: extraction | analysis | generation | classification | transformation | validation
[prompt.parameters].temperature must be float between 0.0 and 2.0
[prompt.parameters].max_tokens must be a positive integer
[prompt.parameters].stop_sequences must be an array (can be empty [])
All variables declared in [prompt.variables] must appear in the template body as {{variable_name}}
All {{variable_name}} references in the template body must be declared in [prompt.variables]
[[examples]] entries must have both 'input' and 'output' fields
[validation].required must be an array of variable names
[[validation.checks]] entries must have: variable, regex, message fields
Regex values in [[validation.checks]] must be valid regex syntax
recommended_sections: ## System Message — Role declaration and primary objective
## Template Body — Context, Request, and Inputs placeholders
[validation] — Variable Validation Rules (required fields, regex checks)

SUBAGENT_TOML._purpose: Mistral-vibe parallel extraction subagent configuration. Controls one node in a chunked file-processing fleet. Output is TOML driving a downstream JSON extraction process.
SUBAGENT_TOML._sre_risk: Hard Boundary Enforcement without Gradation — invalid edge type enum values or non-existent file paths cause extraction pipeline failures. Non-binary confidence scores outside defined ranges produce invalid graph data.

Abstraction Level.directive: All values must be concrete literals. chunk_id and total_chunks must be positive integers. files must be an array of actual relative file paths — no glob patterns, no placeholders. Edge type values must be from the defined enum exactly. Confidence scores must be floats from the defined range table.
Abstraction Level.failure_mode: File paths with glob patterns or placeholders. Non-integer chunk IDs. Abstract descriptions in place of concrete enum values.
Abstraction Level.self_check: Can every value in this TOML be parsed and used directly without interpretation? Are all file paths literal relative strings?

Imagery Density.directive: Comments factual only. Describe what each configuration controls, not what it represents. The extraction domain (graph nodes, semantic similarity, hyperedges) uses precise technical vocabulary — use it without embellishment.
Imagery Density.failure_mode: Comments that describe the graph metaphorically ('weaving the knowledge web') rather than technically ('adds semantically_similar_to edges between nodes with shared problem domain').
Imagery Density.self_check: Would a pipeline engineer understand each comment without interpretation?

Constraint Adherence.directive: Edge relation values must be from this exact list: calls | implements | references | cites | conceptually_related_to | shares_data_with | semantically_similar_to | rationale_for | import | defines | wraps | participate_in | form. Confidence enum must be: EXTRACTED | INFERRED | AMBIGUOUS. Confidence scores must follow the range table: EXTRACTED=1.0, INFERRED_HIGH=0.9, INFERRED_MID=0.7, INFERRED_LOW=0.5, AMBIGUOUS=0.2. File type values in scope.types must be: code | document | paper | image. Subagent role must be: extraction | analysis | verification | execution.
Constraint Adherence.failure_mode: Invented edge type names. Confidence scores outside the defined range table. File type strings not in the enum.
Constraint Adherence.self_check: Is every enum value in this TOML traceable to the defined lists? Is every confidence score within its defined range?

Novelty Injection.directive: Follow subagent-template.md structure exactly. Do not invent new extraction modes, new file handler sections, or new confidence tiers. The extraction behavior sections ([extraction.edge_rules], [extraction.confidence], [extraction.semantic_similarity], [extraction.hyperedges]) have a fixed schema — do not add keys beyond what the template defines.
Novelty Injection.failure_mode: Custom extraction modes not in the template. New confidence tier names. Invented file handler extension lists.
Novelty Injection.self_check: Is every key in this TOML present in the subagent-template.md schema?

Emotional / Rhetorical Intensity.directive: Flat technical language throughout. Comments describe pipeline behavior. No emphasis markers.
Emotional / Rhetorical Intensity.failure_mode: Comments that dramatize the extraction process.
Emotional / Rhetorical Intensity.self_check: Are all comments purely descriptive of pipeline mechanics?

Narrative / Logical Progression.directive: Section order must follow the template: [subagent] → [subagent.scope] → [subagent.mode] → [subagent.output] → [subagent.error_handling] → [extraction.*] → [file_handlers.*] → [context]. Do not reorder sections.
Narrative / Logical Progression.failure_mode: Sections out of template order. Extraction behavior sections appearing before subagent identity sections.
Narrative / Logical Progression.self_check: Does the section order match the subagent-template.md exactly?

recommended_sections: [extraction.confidence] — Confidence Score Table (float values per tier)
[extraction.node_id] — Node ID Schema (format, stem, entity rules)
[file_handlers.code] — Code File Handler (extensions, focus, skip lists)
[context] — Domain Context Injection (domain_hint, prompt, frontmatter propagation)
validation_rules: TOML syntax must be valid
subagent.role must be one of: extraction | analysis | verification | execution
subagent.chunk_id must be a positive integer >= 1
subagent.total_chunks must be a positive integer >= subagent.chunk_id
subagent.scope.files must be a non-empty array of strings
subagent.scope.types values must all be from: code | document | paper | image
subagent.scope.max_files must be a positive integer
subagent.output.format must be 'json'
subagent.output.schema_version must be a non-empty string
All edge names in extraction.edge_rules.extracted must be from the defined edge enum
All edge names in extraction.edge_rules.inferred must be from the defined edge enum
extraction.confidence.extracted must be 1.0
extraction.confidence.inferred_high must be 0.9
extraction.confidence.inferred_mid must be 0.7
extraction.confidence.inferred_low must be 0.5
extraction.confidence.ambiguous must be 0.2
extraction.node_id.format must be a non-empty string
extraction.hyperedges.min_nodes must be an integer >= 3
file_handlers.image.use_vision must be boolean
anti_patterns: confidence score values not in the defined range table
edge type names not in the defined enum
file handler extension lists missing the leading dot (e.g. 'py' instead of '.py')
[file_handlers.*] blocks for handler types not in: code | document | paper | image
Inventing new extraction behavior keys not in the template schema

dimensions.description: Degree to which the output operates at conceptual vs concrete registers.

context_gathering.directive: Stay concrete. Catalog facts, not interpretations. Low abstraction maximizes fidelity to source material.
context_gathering.failure_mode: Premature synthesis — jumping to thematic conclusions before the source is fully mapped.
context_gathering.self_check: Am I cataloging what is there, or what I think it means?

drafting.directive: Move to thematic and conceptual registers where appropriate. But apply file_profile overrides.
drafting.failure_mode: Existential drift — the output describes philosophy instead of function.
drafting.self_check: Does abstraction serve the artifact type, or am I abstracting because I can?

review.directive: Check whether abstractions land. If a claim requires decoder-ring work, demote to concrete.
review.failure_mode: Accepting abstractions that don't earn their complexity.
review.self_check: Does each abstraction clarify or obscure?

dimensions.description: Frequency and vividness of metaphor, analogy, and figurative language.

context_gathering.failure_mode: Metaphorical cataloging — describing the source through figures of speech.
context_gathering.self_check: Am I using the source's language or inventing imagery?

drafting.directive: Apply file_profile register. Technical artifacts: minimal to none. Each metaphor must clarify, not decorate.
drafting.failure_mode: Metaphor accumulation — every sentence becomes a figure of speech.

review.directive: Audit metaphor count and consistency. Cut metaphors that compete or don't land.

dimensions.description: Degree to which the output introduces unexpected connections, framings, or approaches.

context_gathering.directive: Suppress novelty. Note interesting connections, but do not pursue them. Your job is to understand what is there.
context_gathering.failure_mode: Chasing tangents before the map is complete.
context_gathering.self_check: Am I following the source or departing from it?

drafting.directive: Introduce novel connections deliberately and sparingly. Apply file_profile register. Technical artifacts: suppressed (use standard patterns).
drafting.failure_mode: Scope creep — the draft becomes about your additions rather than the synthesis.
drafting.self_check: Is each novel element serving the synthesis?

review.directive: Force one maximum-entropy pass: what is the most unexpected, defensible reframing this material supports?
review.failure_mode: Treating the draft as final because it is coherent, without asking if it is interesting.
review.self_check: What is the most surprising true thing this material supports that the draft has not said?

dimensions.description: How strictly boundaries and requirements are enforced.

context_gathering.directive: Internalize constraints. They are not negotiable. Catalog what is required, forbidden, and in-scope.
context_gathering.failure_mode: Loosening constraints to make the problem easier.
context_gathering.self_check: Have I cataloged all constraints accurately?

drafting.directive: Enforce constraints strictly. Apply file_profile validation rules.
drafting.self_check: For each constraint violation found: deliberate, documented, worth it?

review.directive: Audit all constraints. Binary pass/fail for technical artifacts.
review.self_check: Does this pass all constraint checks?

global_intervention_primitives.too_much_exploration: Impose commit constraint: force a decision, limit alternatives. Cap imagery density.
global_intervention_primitives.too_much_collapse: Force expansion: require N alternatives, inject adversarial framing.
global_intervention_primitives.loop_detected: Break recursion: remove self-referential context, reset to problem statement only.
global_intervention_primitives.constraint_violation_detected: Surface and name the violation before deciding whether to accept or revert it.
global_intervention_primitives.perspective_drift_detected: Re-anchor: restate the synthesis perspective explicitly before continuing.

High Exploration + Weak Constraints.telemetry: Metaphor accumulation; imagery density runaway; every sentence becomes a figure of speech.

Self-Revision Loop.telemetry: Draft enters self-commentary; output discusses its own construction.

Competing Interpretations.telemetry: Document uses more than two distinct metaphor systems; signal contradicts across sections.
